title: amphicarpaea africana
intro: Amphicarpaea africana, also known as the twin-fruit or two-flowered bean, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to tropical Africa. The plant's roots, leaves, and seeds have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Amphicarpaea africana has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Amphicarpaea africana typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures of the plant's roots, leaves, or seeds, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
